seoTitle: Turn Off Sales Tax in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to disabling automated sales tax tracking in QuickBooks Online, including turning off individual tax agencies or the entire engine.
intro: If your business has stopped collecting sales tax — maybe you moved to tax-exempt services or an external platform now handles it — leaving QuickBooks Online's tax engine running just adds clutter to your invoices. This guide covers how to deactivate individual tax agencies or switch off the sales tax feature entirely.

steps.heading: Open the Sales Tax settings
steps.body: From the left navigation, go to Taxes, then the Sales Tax tab to see your active tax agencies and their current status.

steps.heading: Deactivate individual agencies
steps.body: In Sales Tax Settings, find the Turn Off or Deactivate toggle next to a specific agency to stop it from auto-populating new transactions.

steps.heading: Confirm the deactivation
steps.body: A warning prompt appears when you turn off an agency — confirm it to stop QuickBooks from tracking that jurisdiction going forward.

steps.heading: Switch off sales tax globally
steps.body: For a full shutdown, use the master Turn Off Sales Tax button in settings, then save to apply the change across your invoice layouts.

steps.heading: Check customer records
steps.body: Review individual customer profiles to make sure they're marked tax-exempt where needed, then create a test invoice to confirm the tax line no longer appears.

faq.question: Will turning off sales tax delete my past tax records?
faq.answer: No, historical transactions and tax filings remain in your reports — deactivating the feature only stops it from applying to new transactions.

faq.question: Can I turn sales tax back on later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reactivate the sales tax center and reconfigure your agencies if your business needs change again.

faq.question: Do I need to update each customer individually?
faq.answer: It's worth double-checking key customer profiles, since some may have tax-exempt flags that no longer apply once you disable the feature globally.

faq.question: Why does the tax column still show on an old invoice?
faq.answer: Existing invoices keep the tax data they were created with; only new invoices created after the change will reflect the updated settings.
